title: Target residues from the interaction of copper with 20-46 MeV/nucleon 12-C ions

x4freetext: The target assembly used for the irradiation consisted
of eight 18.9 mg/cm2 natural copper foils 99.9% in
purity surrounded by 10.5 mg/cm2 thick Mylar forward
and backward catcher foils. Additional Mylar foils were
inserted between the copper targets to degrade the
incident energies to the desired values.

x4codes.hlp: Institute of Modern Physics, CAS, Lanzhou, China, People`s Republic

x4freetext: The irradiation lasted for about 18 hours, with
typical beam intensity of 4 - 8 electrical nA.
The measurement of gamma-rays was started
25 min. after the end of the irradiation and lasted for
The uncertainty in the thickness of the copper target
is negligible, the uncertainty in beam current
The errors listed are standard deviations,
including errors from analysis of the gamma-ray
spectra, from resolution of the decay curves by
